title: Discoverable Agent Knowledge -- A Formal Framework for Agentic KG Affordances (Extended Version)
summary:
arXiv:2605.19186v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Two decades ago, the Semantic Web Services community was asked how agents with different ontological commitments could discover, compose, and invoke web services coherently. The response was OWL-S and WSMO: formally grounded capability descriptions specifying what a service could do, what the agent must already know for invocation to be epistemically sound, and how ontological mismatches could be formally bridged.
